markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Minion Guise](/Feats.aspx?ID=2085) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
<trait label="Social" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=325" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 196

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
animal companion or familiar; Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

</column>

---

When you are in your social identity, you can also grant a social identity to an animal companion, familiar, or other minion you gained from a class feature. When changing to your social identity, you also change your minion's appearance to that of a socially acceptable creature of its type, such as grooming a wolf to appear as a large dog or disguising a familiar to appear as an exotic pet. Commanding your minion to use unusual magical or combat abilities it gained from your class features or feats while in this social identity risks exposing your vigilante identity.
name: Minion Guise
prerequisite_markdown: animal companion or familiar; Expert in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Safe House](/Feats.aspx?ID=2086) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 196

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) A vigilante's Safe House, can be located in any city or near any lodge they have received a Pathfinder briefing. A vigilante can use their downtime to relocate their safe house. A vigilante can use their safe house only if it is in the town or location where their current adventure is taking place._

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

</column>

---

You establish a safe house—a secure space in which to hide your secrets from the outside world. This safe house is roughly the size of a 10-foot cube. It's in a location you have access to, and it can be part of a larger building or structure, like a hidden room or an underground cave. The safe house protects objects and people inside it from magical detection. This has the effects of [_nondetection_](/Spells.aspx?ID=209), using your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) DC and half your level rounded up for the counteract level. Setting up or moving your safe house takes a week of downtime. The size of the safe house expands to four 10-foot cubes if you're an expert in Deception, eight cubes if you're a master, and 16 cubes if you're legendary.
name: Safe House

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Social Purview](/Feats.aspx?ID=2087) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 196

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

</column>

---

You have built a reputation for yourself in your social identity. Choose one archetype that you meet the prerequisites for. You gain that archetype's dedication feat and can select feats from that archetype, even if you haven't yet gained enough feats in the vigilante archetype to take another dedication feat. These feats become part of your social identity and gain the social trait—for instance, a [fighter](/Classes.aspx?ID=7) vigilante could take the [wizard dedication](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=12) feat and have a [wizard](/Classes.aspx?ID=12) social identity. Using these feats in your social identity doesn't risk exposing your vigilante identity, but using them in your vigilante identity could put you at risk for exposure.
name: Social Purview

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Startling Appearance (Vigilante)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2088) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />
<trait label="Fear" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=68" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
<trait label="Vigilante" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=326" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 197

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
[Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

**Requirements**
You are completely [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by the target creature.

</column>

---

You can startle foes who are unaware of your presence. Make a Strike against your target. That creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against this Strike, as normal. If your Strike hits, the target remains flat-footed for the rest of your turn and is [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) (frightened 2 on a critical hit).

<title level="2">Startling Appearance (Vigilante) leads to...</title>
[Frightening Appearance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2092), [Stunning Appearance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2093)
name: Startling Appearance (Vigilante)
requirement_markdown: You are completely [unnoticed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=41) by the target creature.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

</column>

Your disparate identities allow you to defeat magic that detects lies. As long as what you say is true from the point of view of your current identity, you can say it even under effects like [_zone of truth_](/Spells.aspx?ID=379) that force you to speak the truth.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Many Guises](/Feats.aspx?ID=2091) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 197

**Archetype** [Vigilante](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=80)

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5); [Vigilante Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2083)

</column>

---

You can take on any number of mundane guises. Whenever you change your identity, instead of taking on your social or vigilante identity, you can become someone completely ordinary. This identity isn't a specific individual—rather, you become a nondescript member of your ancestry, of any gender, with a neutral alignment and a mundane occupation such as common laborer, farmer, or peasant. Spells and abilities detect you as if you were this ordinary identity, rather than either of your two real identities, unless they succeed at a counteract check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC. You can't use either social abilities or vigilante abilities while in this identity.
name: Many Guises

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Viking Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2094) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 198

**Archetype** [Viking](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=81)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

Vikings spend long periods of time at sea, only to leap from their boats at a moment's notice, charge through the surf, and take their enemies by storm. You're trained in [Sailing Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8). When moving through water using your land Speed, you ignore difficult terrain resulting from shallow water, such as in bogs or on beaches. In addition, if you roll a success for your [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=39), you get a critical success instead.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the viking archetype.

<title level="2">Viking Dedication leads to...</title>
[Hurling Charge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2095), [Second Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=2098), [Viking Vindicator](/Feats.aspx?ID=3619), [Viking Weapon Familiarity](/Feats.aspx?ID=2096)
name: Viking Dedication

You are trained with the [battle axe](/Weapons.aspx?ID=16), [hatchet](/Weapons.aspx?ID=27), [longsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=31), and [shortsword](/Weapons.aspx?ID=43). In addition, you gain the [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) reaction.

>
[Second Shield](/Feats.aspx?ID=2098) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 198

**Archetype** [Viking](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=81)

**Prerequisites**
[Viking Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2094)

**Trigger**
Your [Shield Block](/Feats.aspx?ID=839) causes your shield to break or be destroyed.

</column>

---

You're used to your shield breaking in the middle of battle, and you're prepared to use a backup or any convenient nearby object to defend yourself. You can Interact to draw a shield on your person or an unattended shield within your reach. If there is an object within your reach that could serve as an improvised shield—for example, a table or chair— you can Interact to draw it with this feat. The GM determines if something can be used as an improvised shield. Your new shield isn't raised until you use the [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98) action, as normal.
name: Second Shield

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Weapon Improviser Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2099) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 199

**Archetype** [Weapon Improviser](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=82)

**Prerequisites**
trained in all martial weapons

</column>

---

You don't take the normal –2 penalty to attack rolls with [improvised weapons](/Rules.aspx?ID=224). Additionally, whenever you gain a class feature that grants you expert or greater proficiency with any weapon, you also gain that proficiency with improvised weapons.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the weapon improviser archetype.

<title level="2">Weapon Improviser Dedication leads to...</title>
[Improvised Critical](/Feats.aspx?ID=2102), [Improvised Pummel](/Feats.aspx?ID=2100), [Makeshift Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2103), [Surprise Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2101)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Improvised Pummel](/Feats.aspx?ID=2100) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 199

**Archetype** [Weapon Improviser](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=82)

**Prerequisites**
[Weapon Improviser Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2099)

**Requirements**
You are wielding an improvised weapon.

</column>

---

You make a Strike with your wielded improvised weapon. You gain a +1 item bonus to the attack roll, and the Strike deals two weapon damage dice if it would have dealt fewer. If the attack is a critical hit, in addition to the effect of the critical hit, the improvised weapon breaks. If the item has a Hardness greater than your level, or if it's an [artifact](/Equipment.aspx?Category=45), [cursed item](/Equipment.aspx?Category=49), or other item that's difficult to break or destroy, the item doesn't break and the attack is a hit instead of a critical hit.

 At 12th level, your item bonus to the attack roll increases to +2 instead of +1, and at 16th level, the Strike deals three weapon damage dice if it would have dealt fewer, instead of two.

<title level="2">Improvised Pummel leads to...</title>
[Shattering Strike (Weapon Improviser)](/Feats.aspx?ID=2104)
name: Improvised Pummel
requirement_markdown: You are wielding an improvised weapon.

    right="Feat 11"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[A Home in Every Port](/Feats.aspx?ID=2105) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Downtime" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=49" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 202

**Prerequisites**
Charisma 16

</column>

---

You have a reputation in towns and villages you've visited, and residents are always willing to open their doors to you. When in a town or village, during downtime, you can spend 8 hours to locate a resident willing to provide lodging for you and up to six allies for up to 24 hours at no charge. [The standard of living within the acquired lodging is comfortable, and square meals are provided at no cost](/Equipment.aspx?Category=2). After 24 hours, you must pay standard prices for further lodging and meals or use this feat again to find a new resident willing to host you.
name: A Home in Every Port

</title>

<traits>

<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />

Master in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

You use your medical training to ameliorate sickness or assuage fears. When you use [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) to [Administer First Aid](/Actions.aspx?ID=54), instead of Stabilizing a character or Stopping Bleeding, you can reduce an ally's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) or [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34) condition by 2, or remove either of those conditions entirely on a critical success. You can remove only one condition at a time. The DC for the Medicine check is usually the DC of the effect that caused the condition.
name: Advanced First Aid

    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Aerobatics Mastery](/Feats.aspx?ID=2108) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

Master in [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1)

</column>

---

You move with grace in flight and can perform amazing aerial stunts. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) checks to [Maneuver in Flight](/Actions.aspx?ID=30) and can combine two maneuvers into a single action, such as reversing direction while making a steep ascent or descent or hovering in gale-force winds. The DC of the Acrobatics check is equal to the DC of the most difficult maneuver + 5. If you're legendary in Acrobatics, you can combine three such maneuvers into a single action; the DC of the Acrobatics check is equal to the DC of the most difficult maneuver + 10. Regardless of the combination, these maneuvers rarely allow you to move farther than your fly Speed.
name: Aerobatics Mastery

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Armor Assist](/Feats.aspx?ID=2109) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 203

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The DCs for the Armor Assist feat are not subject to GM adjudication. Rather, simply use the default DCs listed in the feat: 15 for common armor, 20 for uncommon armor, and 25 for rare armor._

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) or Trained in [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

After your service aiding armored combatants, you are practiced in helping yourself and others don heavy gear. You can attempt an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) or [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check with a DC determined by the GM (but usually 15 for common armor, DC 20 for uncommon armor, and DC 25 for rare armor) to halve the time you take to don armor. You can halve an ally's time to don armor by working with them to don the armor and succeeding at an Athletics or Warfare Lore check against the same DC.
name: Armor Assist
prerequisite_markdown: Trained in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) or Trained in [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

You have learned techniques to adjust and modify your armor and movements to reduce the noise you make. When you wear non-[noisy](/Traits.aspx?ID=205) armor with which you are trained, your penalty to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks is reduced by 1 (to a minimum penalty of 0). If you're a master in Stealth, reduce the penalty by 2, and if you're legendary, reduce the penalty by 3. If your armor has the noisy trait, instead of reducing the penalty to Stealth checks, you ignore the effects of the noisy trait, enabling you to remove the penalty with a sufficient Strength score as normal.
name: Armored Stealth

<title

    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Assured Identification](/Feats.aspx?ID=2111) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 203

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You rarely misidentify an item. When using [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) checks to [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=24), if you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead. If you would [misidentify a cursed item because you roll a success but not a critical success](/Rules.aspx?ID=1094), you simply can't identify it instead.
name: Assured Identification
prerequisite_markdown: Expert in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Expert in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Expert in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Battle Planner](/Feats.aspx?ID=2112) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 203

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8)

</column>

---

You are constantly drawing up plans and battle scenarios, assembling strategies and gathered intelligence for later use. When you scout an enemy's position or receive a detailed report from an ally who scouted the enemy's position, if you have a clear indication of the number, position, and identities of your potential foes, you can spend 1 minute to come up with a battle plan that takes such potential factors into account and reduces the role luck plays in the equation. Roll a [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) check. As long as the information was accurate and remains accurate when you roll initiative against those enemies, you can use the Warfare Lore result you previously rolled for your initiative roll; if you do, this is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=76) effect.
name: Battle Planner

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 203 **Prerequisites** Master in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) --- In even a brief conversation or social interaction, you pick up on subtle social and visual cues to learn a great deal about a person's origin and history. You might notice bits of green under the person's fingernails and determine they're an herbalist, a pin indicating their membership in a secret society, or something similar. You pick up on only details that have to do with their societal role, so you might learn the city district where a [vampire](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97) lives, but wouldn't learn any of their weaknesses, nor necessarily even that they are a vampire. Spend 1 minute in the presence of someone you haven't met before, or haven't met since you first gained Biographical Eye, then attempt a DC 30 [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the check if you engaged the person in conversation during this time. If the person is deliberately trying to conceal their nature or present a false identity, you learn about their false biography rather than their true one unless the result of your Society check exceeds their Will DC. **Critical Success** You learn the creature's profession, their specialty within that profession, and a major accomplishment or controversy from their career. You also learn the nation and settlement where they live, as well as the district in a city large enough to have districts. In addition, you learn the nation or settlement where they spent their formative years.
**Success** You learn the creature's profession and specialty within that profession. You learn the nation or settlement where they normally live.
**Failure** You learn the creature's profession and the region of the world they hail from, but no more.
**Critical Failure** You learn a piece of erroneous information about the creature.
name: Biographical Eye

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 203 **Prerequisites** Trained in [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) --- You launch an insightful quip at a foe, distracting them. Choose a foe within 30 feet and roll a [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) check against the target's Will DC. **Critical Success** The target is distracted and takes a –3 status penalty to Perception and Will saves for 1 minute. The target can end the effect early with a retort to your Bon Mot. This can either be a single action that has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait or an appropriate skill action to frame their retort. The GM determines which skill actions qualify, though they must take at least 1 action. Typically, the retort needs to use a [linguistic](/Traits.aspx?ID=101) Charisma-based skill action.
**Success** As critical success, but the penalty is –2.
**Critical Failure** Your quip is atrocious. You take the same penalty an enemy would take had you succeeded. This ends after 1 minute or if you issue another Bon Mot and succeed.
name: Bon Mot
search_markdown:

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Emotion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=60" />

markdown:
**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 204 **Frequency** once per day **Prerequisites** Master in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Master in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) --- You have learned rites or meditations that enable you to perceive minor, invisible spirits within a place. Choose [Nature](/Domains.aspx?ID=22), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) when you select this feat. Nature allows you to contact the spirits of nature that form [leshies](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=67), who are born of pure life essence rather than spiritual energy and can answer questions about natural features like the location of nearby water or plant life. Religion reveals the presence of [angelic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=3), [demonic](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=28), or other spirits in service to divine beings, who provide information about sources of powerful positive or negative energy, sacred or profane influences, or the presence of undead. Occultism allows you to contact lingering spirits, psychic echoes of the departed dead, and spirits from beyond reality, who tell you about things like strange auras, effects, or the presence of unnatural occult beings. Spend 10 minutes and attempt a check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) with the chosen skill; the DC is determined by the GM (usually a very high DC for the level of the highest-level creature you might encounter in the area). If you're legendary in the chosen skill, you can use this feat once per hour, instead of only once per day, but you can't use it again on any area that overlaps a previous area. **Special** You can select this feat multiple times, each time picking a different skill in which you have the master proficiency rank. You can use this feat with each skill once per day (or once per hour, if you're legendary). **Critical Success** The spirits reveal themselves to you and have a [helpful](/Conditions.aspx?ID=21) attitude toward you. Only you can perceive these spirits. They answer three simple questions about the environment within 100 feet of you, depending on the skill you chose and thus the type of spirits you contact. Their answers are almost always a single word, and their knowledge is limited to within their area of interest.
**Success** As with a critical success, but the spirits are [indifferent](/Conditions.aspx?ID=25) to you and answer only one question.
**Failure** You are unable to contact the spirits of this place.
**Critical Failure** You contact one or more malevolent spirits. They are [hostile](/Conditions.aspx?ID=23) toward you, though they may not immediately appear so. They answer up to three questions but give you information that is harmful to you in some way, as determined by the GM.
name: Consult the Spirits
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Master in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

You have dealings with a variety of unsavory characters, which you can leverage to trade favors or meet powerful people. When you're in an area where you have connections (typically a settlement where you've spent downtime building connections or possibly another area in the same nation), you can attempt a [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) check to arrange a meeting with an important criminal, such as a thieves' guild leader, or ask for a favor in exchange for a later favor of your contact's choice. The GM decides the DC based on the difficulty of the favor and the figure's prominence.

Even those who don't understand your language are unsettled by these dire secrets. Attempt an [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) check and compare the result to the Will DC of an enemy within 30 feet, or to the Will DCs of any number of enemies within 30 feet if you are legendary in Occultism. Those creatures are temporarily immune for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The target becomes [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=5) for 1 round and [frightened 1](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19).
**Success** The target becomes frightened 1.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** You get overly caught up in your own words and become frightened 1.
name: Disturbing Knowledge

You disguise your true meaning behind other words and phrases, relying on subtle emphasis and shared experience to convey meaning that only your allies understand. Any allies who have traveled alongside you for at least 1 full week automatically discern your meaning. Other observers must succeed at a Perception check against your [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC to realize you are passing a secret message, and they must critically succeed to understand the message itself.

When you [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) something of or [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=52) members of your own faith, you can attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check instead of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) or [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7), and you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to the check. On a critically failed attempt to make a Request, the target's attitude toward you doesn't worsen.

When calculating your [travel speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=470) for the day while mounted, you can attempt a [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10) check to [Command an Animal](/Actions.aspx?ID=58) to increase your mount's travel speed. The DC is determined by the GM, but is typically based on the mount's level or the difficulty of the environment, whichever is harder. On a success, increase your mount's travel speed by half. This has no effect on your mount's movement in encounters.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eye for Numbers](/Feats.aspx?ID=2127) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 206

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You've learned to subitize, quickly estimating the number of items in a group with relative accuracy at only a glance. You immediately learn the number of visually similar items in a group you can see (such as coins, books, or people), rounded to the first digit in the total number. For example, you could look at a case of potion vials and learn that it held about 30 vials, but you wouldn't know that it was exactly 33 vials, how many different types of potions there were, or how many of which type. Similarly, you could look at a pile of 2,805 coins and know that there were about 3,000 coins in all. You can use this ability only on items that can typically be counted, so you can't use it on grains of sand or stars in the sky, for example.

 In addition, when you attempt to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) that is primarily numerical or mathematical, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check.
name: Eye for Numbers

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Forensic Acumen](/Feats.aspx?ID=2128) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 206

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

You understand the principles of forensic medicine, making you better at examining a body to determine the cause of death or injury. You can perform a forensic examination on a body, as described under [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) in the [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) skill, in half the normal amount of time it would take (to a minimum of 5 minutes). If you succeed at your check, you can attempt an immediate check to Recall Knowledge to follow up on something you found, with a +2 circumstance bonus. This check is usually related to the cause of injury or death, such as a [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4) check to identify a poison or weapon that was used or an additional Medicine check to identify a specific disease. If you prefer, you can instead attempt to Recall Knowledge about the type of creature whose body you were examining, using the appropriate skill and gaining the same circumstance bonus.

 The circumstance bonus increases to +3 if you have master proficiency in Medicine and +4 if you have legendary proficiency.
name: Forensic Acumen

<title

    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glean Contents](/Feats.aspx?ID=2129) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 206

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14)

</column>

---

You are adept at quickly scanning loose papers and carefully discerning the contents of sealed letters without damaging the seal. You can attempt [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) checks to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=22) on a message that is only partially glimpsed, upside down or reversed from your perspective, or even sealed. However, on a critical failure, the recipient is made aware of your efforts (for instance, you damage the seal or disturb the papers in some way). When using this feat to decipher sealed letters, your attempt to Decipher Writing gains the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. This feat doesn't prevent witnesses from noticing your efforts, particularly with sealed letters which you must manipulate directly in order to read; you might need to attempt [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) checks to avoid being noticed.
name: Glean Contents

You are skilled at making quick fixes to damaged equipment, but your fixes aren't meant to last forever. You make a quick repair to a [broken](/Conditions.aspx?ID=2) non-magical item in your possession. Until the item takes damage again, you can still use it as a [shoddy item](/Rules.aspx?ID=197) of its type. This repair restores no Hit Points, so the item is easy to destroy. Once the item is [Repaired](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) normally such that it is no longer broken, it is also no longer shoddy.

</column>

---

With patience and time, you can make bird calls, leave game trails, and ultimately influence the behavior of a certain type of animals in the region to favor and even aid you in the days to come. The GM determines the DC of any check required and the amount of time your work requires (usually at least a day or two of downtime). While you can't directly control how you've influenced nature, you can hope for certain effects, such as easier hunts or birds falling silent whenever danger is approaching. If you're legendary in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), you can elicit these same adjustments to animal behavior in the area by spending only 10 minutes.
name: Influence Nature

This doesn't increase your party's Speed during an encounter or allow your party to ignore difficult terrain during an encounter.

    pfs="Standard"

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You carry a small token of protection from a site holy to your faith, or you touched your religious symbol to a relic or altar at such a site. So long as this token is in your possession, when you tie an adversary's initiative roll, you go first.

**Special** If you select this feat at 1st level, you receive your pilgrim's token for free. Alternately, if you have a religious symbol, it is already attuned, as described above.

 If you select this feat at a later level, or if you lose your pilgrim's token, you must purchase or [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) a replacement and attune it at a holy site. Such a token usually costs at least 2 sp, and the attunement takes 10 minutes of prayer and requires a successful DC 20 [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check. Your GM might adjust the price and DC depending on the token's material and quality and the religious significance of the site; the more significant the location, the easier the attunement.
name: Pilgrim's Token

the Price of the chosen item

**Requirements**
You haven't used this ability since the last time you were able to purchase goods.

</column>

---

You regularly create convoluted plans and contingencies, using your resources to enact them. You take 1 minute to remove your [backpack](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4), then carefully remove an item you hadn't previously declared that you purchased—you intuited that you would come to need the item and purchased it at the latest opportunity. The item must be a piece of [adventuring gear](/Equipment.aspx?Category=1), and can't be a weapon, armor, alchemical item, magic item, or other treasure. It must be common with a level no higher than half your level, and its Bulk must be low enough that carrying it wouldn't have made you [encumbered](/Conditions.aspx?ID=12).

<title level="2">Prescient Planner leads to...</title>
[Prescient Consumable](/Feats.aspx?ID=2142)

Your talismans ward against foul magic. During your daily preparations, you can assemble a small pouch with bits of herbs, hair, sacred oils, and other ritual ingredients, which you give to one ally. The first time that day the ally attempts a saving throw against a spell or [haunt](/Traits.aspx?ID=88), they gain a +1 circumstance bonus to the roll. This bonus increases to +2 if you're an expert in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) or +3 if you're legendary.

You notice the signs and symbols that members of mystery cults and other secret societies use to declare their affiliation to fellow members. You can use [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) in place of [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) about such groups. If you belong to a secret cult, lodge, sect, or similar organization, you automatically recognize members of your group unless they are specifically attempting to conceal their presence from you.

You have learned special tricks that help you follow individuals without them noticing you. When you attempt a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) check to [Avoid Notice](/Actions.aspx?ID=511) while following a specific target, the target takes a –2 circumstance penalty to their Perception DC. If you have master proficiency in Stealth, the penalty is –3 or –4 if you're legendary. If you start an encounter with the target while shadowing them, the target takes this penalty to their initiative roll and to their Perception DC to determine if they notice you, as normal for [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63).
name: Shadow Mark

    level="1"

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Supertaster](/Feats.aspx?ID=2152) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
</traits>

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 209

**Prerequisites**
master in Perception

</column>

---

You have refined your palate and have a discerning sense of taste that can detect abnormalities in the flavor and texture of food and beverages. When eating food or drinking a beverage, you automatically attempt to identify the ingredients, which might alert you to the presence of alterations or additives, such as [poisons](/Traits.aspx?ID=126). The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) Perception check using the poison's level to determine the DC; on a success, you learn that the food or drink was poisoned, but not the specific poison used. 

If you lick or taste something while [Investigating](/Actions.aspx?ID=516) or attempting to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify something, if the taste would provide relevant additional information (at the GM's discretion), you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your check.
name: Supertaster

You take your time searching to ensure you find everything. When [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519), you can take twice as long to search. Normally this means you Search at up to one quarter of your Speed, to a maximum of 150 feet per minute to check everything, or 75 feet per minute to check everything before you walk into it. If you do, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Perception checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=84).

You are constantly under the effects of a 6th-level [_true seeing_](/Spells.aspx?ID=344) spell, using your Perception modifier for the [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) check.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Underground Network](/Feats.aspx?ID=2156) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 209

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14); [Streetwise](/Feats.aspx?ID=846)

</column>

---

You're connected to groups that know what's going on in the streets, and you can get information out of them quickly. When you use [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14) to [Gather Information](/Actions.aspx?ID=49) in an area where you have a network (typically a settlement where you've spent at least a week or spent a day of downtime to build a network faster), you can contact a member of these groups to get information directly from them. This usually takes about an hour, and it doesn't draw as much attention as Gathering Information in public might. The check and information gained otherwise follow the normal rules for Gather Information.

 In addition, if you have successfully consulted the underground network, you get a +1 circumstance bonus to the next check to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) you attempt about the subject you were Gathering Information on, or a +2 circumstance bonus if you're using [Underworld Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) for the check. The GM might change the Lore skill related to the network depending on your location or the specifics of the network you're tapping into.
name: Underground Network

    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Water Sprint](/Feats.aspx?ID=2157) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

**Source** [Advanced Player's Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=39) pg. 209

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3)

</column>

---

Experience and training have taught you that water has just enough surface tension for a master sprinter to traverse the surface. When you Stride in a straight line, if you move at least half your Speed over ground, you can move any amount of the remaining distance across a level body of water. If you don't end your Stride on solid ground, you fall into the water. 

If you're legendary in [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3), as long as you start on solid ground, any part of your Stride can cross the water's surface, even if you aren't moving a straight line, though you still fall into the water if you don't end your movement on solid ground.
name: Water Sprint

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Artokus's Fire](/Feats.aspx?ID=2158) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Additive 2" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=4" />
<trait label="Alchemist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=7" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 20

**Frequency**
once per round

**Trigger**
You use [Quick Alchemy](/Actions.aspx?ID=2) to craft an [alchemist's fire](/Equipment.aspx?ID=75), and that bomb's level is at least 2 levels lower than your advanced alchemy level.

</column>

---

Artokus developed a mixture of volatile materials that burns hotter and longer than typical alchemist's fire. You include this additive mixture when crafting your alchemist's fire. The alchemist's fire deals 1d4 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) in addition to what the alchemist fire would normally cause (for instance, a moderate alchemist's fire deals 1d4+2 persistent fire damage). A creature taking persistent fire damage from the alchemist's fire requires two successful flat checks to end the persistent damage, or one check if the creature had an assisted recovery that reduces the flat check to DC 10. The enhanced alchemist's fire burns even underwater, and any attempts to end the persistent damage by using water have no effect.

<title level="2">Artokus's Discoveries</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 20

Artokus Kirran's legacy includes far more than just the sun orchid elixir. As one of the longest-lived alchemists in Golarion, he's developed a number of other significant alchemical discoveries known across the Inner Sea region. The following are some of his more notable discoveries. Artokus is cautious about sharing his alchemical techniques and typically only does so with his most trusted students.

**Related Feats**: [Wish Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=2159)
name: Artokus's Fire

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wish Alchemy](/Feats.aspx?ID=2159) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Alchemist" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=7" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 20

</column>

---

You have learned Artokus Kirran's most hidden secrets, granting you the ability to create alchemy infused with the power of wishes. Once per day when you use advanced alchemy during your daily preparations, you can spend a batch of infused reagents to create a wish vial containing a single common [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1) spell of your choice of 8th level or lower. The spell must have a casting time of no more than 3 actions, no Cost, and must be able to target you. Only you can Activate the wish vial, which takes the same number of Interact actions as the spell's casting time and grants you the effects of the spell.

<title level="2">Artokus's Discoveries</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 20

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 17"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Azaersi's Roads](/Feats.aspx?ID=2160) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Hobgoblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=268" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) For the purposes of Society play, the Azaersi’s Roads feat (page 28) can be used twice per Society adventure—once to travel from the Material Plane to the Plane of Earth, once to travel from the Plane of Earth to the Material Plane._

</column>

---

Azaersi has granted you limited access to the Stone Roads, attuning you to the tiniest sliver of the _Onyx Key_. You gain [_plane shift_](/Spells.aspx?ID=222) as a primal innate spell. You can cast it twice per week. This can be used only to travel back and forth between the [Plane of Earth](/Planes.aspx?ID=5) and the [Material Plane](/Planes.aspx?ID=1). Due to your attunement to the _Onyx Key_, you can act as the spell focus, and you do not require a tuning fork.

<title level="2">The General's Armory</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

Azaersi believes in rewarding loyalty and service, and she is always in search of reliable and trustworthy agents. Sensible officers, clever spies, and trade negotiators who show skill at dealing with non-hobgoblins are all likely to win General Azaersi's favor, and rewards that only she can offer.

**Related Feats**: [Flamboyant Cruelty](/Feats.aspx?ID=2161)
name: Azaersi's Roads

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Hobgoblin" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=268" />
</traits>

<additional-info>
**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

</additional-info>

---

Azaersi has granted you limited access to the Stone Roads, attuning you to the tiniest sliver of the _Onyx Key_.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Flamboyant Cruelty](/Feats.aspx?ID=2161) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=321" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

</column>

---

You love to kick your enemies when they're down, and you look fabulous when you do. When you make a melee weapon Strike against a foe that has at least two of the following conditions, you gain a circumstance bonus to your damage roll equal to the number of these conditions the foe has. The qualifying conditions are [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3), [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), and [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37). If you hit such a foe, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to skill checks to [Tumble Through](/Actions.aspx?ID=29) and to perform your style's panache-granting actions until the end of your turn.

<title level="2">The General's Armory</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 28

Azaersi believes in rewarding loyalty and service, and she is always in search of reliable and trustworthy agents. Sensible officers, clever spies, and trade negotiators who show skill at dealing with non-hobgoblins are all likely to win General Azaersi's favor, and rewards that only she can offer.

**Related Feats**: [Azaersi's Roads](/Feats.aspx?ID=2160)
name: Flamboyant Cruelty

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Restricted"
>
[Achaekek's Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2162) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 58

**Archetype** [Red Mantis Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=17)

**Prerequisites**
[Basic Red Mantis Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=896)

</column>

---

You sense if a creature you have slain in the last year has been restored to life, as long as you and that creature are on the same plane. You also gain the [_Achaekek's clutch_](/Spells.aspx?ID=814) focus spell. If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) by praying to [Achaekek](/Deities.aspx?ID=29) or researching your assigned kill.

<title level="2">Achaekek's Grip leads to...</title>
[Vernai Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2165)

<title level="2">Secrets of the Vernai</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 57

The Red Mantis Assassin archetype appears on page 71 of the _Lost Omens World Guide_. Of the additional options below, only Prayer Attack is available freely to Red Mantis assassins without prior permission from the Blood Mistress. Those who dare learn these rare abilities without her permission run the risk of being cast out of the society and hunted down by their former allies. In theory, a daring infiltration of the Crimson Citadel could allow one to study hidden texts and learn these techniques, but the chambers within and below this fortress are among the most dangerous in the region, so such an attempt should not be made lightly!

**Related Feats**: [Fading](/Feats.aspx?ID=2163), [Prayer Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=2164), [Vernai Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2165)
name: Achaekek's Grip

You are targeted with a Strike by someone you can detect while your Crimson Shroud is active.

---

You fade partially from reality. The attacker must attempt a DC 11 flat check; on a failure, the attack passes harmlessly through the image of your body that remains behind. An instant later you return to reality, and your Crimson Shroud ends.

Those who dare learn these rare abilities without her permission run the risk of being cast out of the society and hunted down by their former allies. In theory, a daring infiltration of the Crimson Citadel could allow one to study hidden texts and learn these techniques, but the chambers within and below this fortress are among the most dangerous in the region, so such an attempt should not be made lightly!

**Related Feats**: [Achaekek's Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2162), [Prayer Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=2164), [Vernai Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2165)

>
[Prayer Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=2164) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 58

**Archetype** [Red Mantis Assassin](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=17)

**Prerequisites**
[Red Mantis Assassin Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=895)

**Requirements**
You are wielding a [sawtooth saber](/Weapons.aspx?ID=65) in each hand.

</column>

---

You've mastered the signature assassination style of the Red Mantis. Attempt to [Feint](/Actions.aspx?ID=48) an enemy within 30 feet. If your Feint is successful, when you use Prayer Attack on subsequent turns you automatically make the target [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) against your melee attacks for that turn without rolling a check to Feint, so as long as you remain visible to the target and the target remains within 30 feet of you. If you use your Prayer Attack against a different target, you must attempt to Feint the target normally.

 When you use Prayer Attack, your next successful Strike with a sawtooth saber that turn deals 2d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) to the target.

<title level="2">Secrets of the Vernai</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 57

Of the additional options below, only Prayer Attack is available freely to Red Mantis assassins without prior permission from the Blood Mistress. Those who dare learn these rare abilities without her permission run the risk of being cast out of the society and hunted down by their former allies. In theory, a daring infiltration of the Crimson Citadel could allow one to study hidden texts and learn these techniques, but the chambers within and below this fortress are among the most dangerous in the region, so such an attempt should not be made lightly!

**Related Feats**: [Achaekek's Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2162), [Fading](/Feats.aspx?ID=2163), [Vernai Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2165)

>
[Vernai Training](/Feats.aspx?ID=2165) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>

[Achaekek's Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2162); [Advanced Red Mantis Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=897)

</column>

---

To resurrect a creature you've slain, a spellcaster must [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) your influence on its death. The DC of this check is equal to your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. In addition, add two 5th-level spells to your Red Mantis assassin spellbook—both spells must be chosen from the following options: [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), [_drop dead_](/Spells.aspx?ID=92), [_false vision_](/Spells.aspx?ID=109), [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=144), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=161), [_mind probe_](/Spells.aspx?ID=193), or [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=272). You gain a 5th-level spell slot that you can use to prepare a spell from your Red Mantis assassin spellbook.

<title level="2">Secrets of the Vernai</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 57

Those who dare learn these rare abilities without her permission run the risk of being cast out of the society and hunted down by their former allies. In theory, a daring infiltration of the Crimson Citadel could allow one to study hidden texts and learn these techniques, but the chambers within and below this fortress are among the most dangerous in the region, so such an attempt should not be made lightly!

**Related Feats**: [Achaekek's Grip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2162), [Fading](/Feats.aspx?ID=2163), [Prayer Attack](/Feats.aspx?ID=2164)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Janatimo's Lessons](/Feats.aspx?ID=2166) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 61

**Archetype** [Magaambyan Attendant](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=29)

**Prerequisites**
[Uzunjati Recollection](/Feats.aspx?ID=1114)

</column>

---

Thanks to Janatimo's secret techniques, you can weave your knowledge and words into powerful tools to assist your allies in life-threatening situations. When you use [Uzunjati Recollection](/Feats.aspx?ID=1114) to tell a story and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) about a creature during a combat encounter, if you succeed at both checks, your story carries deeper meaning. This grants your allies a +1 circumstance bonus to their next attack roll and saving throw against the creature during that encounter, as well as to their AC against that creature's next attack roll during that encounter. If you critically succeed at both checks, the bonuses also apply to all attack rolls, saves, and AC against that creature for 2 rounds.

<title level="2">Janatimo's Secret Lessons</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 61

As a storyteller, Janatimo loves sharing his knowledge with others and helping expand their worldviews, so most of his innovations have passed on to the Uzunjati or the world at large. But there are a few small things he keeps to himself and his closest friends and students, among those a special Uzunjati storytelling technique that could be used to harm the innocent if it fell into the wrong hands.

**Related Feats**:
name: Janatimo's Lessons

</column>

---

Any shield you wield gains the [shove](/Traits.aspx?ID=193) trait. When you [Shove](/Actions.aspx?ID=38) using a shield and Stride as part of that action, you can move an additional 5 feet to either side of the creature you Shoved. In addition, if you are in the [Everstand Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=1087) and successfully Shove a creature using a shield that you wield two-handed, you can increase the distance of your Shove to 10 feet.

<title level="2">Knights in Training</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 68

The following feats represent benefits of training from the Iomedars (Kalabrynne for knight vigilant, Clarethe for knight reclaimant, either for Lastwall sentry).

**Related Feats**: [Shall Not Falter, Shall Not Rout](/Feats.aspx?ID=2168), [Vigil's Walls Rise Anew!](/Feats.aspx?ID=2169)

If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can regain using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity to recite the Crimson Oath and meditate on its teachings. If you already have a focus pool, increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Your knight reclaimant focus spells are divine spells; when you gain this feat, you become trained in divine spell attacks and spell DCs and your spellcasting ability is Charisma.

>

<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

[Knight Vigilant](/Feats.aspx?ID=1092)

</column>

---

As part of daily preparation, you can drill with a number of allies up to your Charisma modifier. These allies must be present and listening during this time, but this does not otherwise impede their own preparation. When you use this feat's benefit, you and all drilled companions within 30 feet who are wielding shields [Raise a Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=98). Allies who Raise their Shields keep them raised until the start of their next turn, as normal.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Medical Researcher](/Feats.aspx?ID=2170) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 72

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

Kassi has taught you not only how to apply medicines with your medical skills but how to craft them as well. When you [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=43) [healer's kits](/Equipment.aspx?ID=25), [vaccines](/Equipment.aspx?ID=773), [addiction suppressants](/Equipment.aspx?ID=774), [antidotes](/Equipment.aspx?ID=80), [antiplagues](/Equipment.aspx?ID=81), [elixirs of life](/Equipment.aspx?ID=91), or other non-magical medical or healing items, you can use [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) instead of [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4).

<title level="2">Kassi's Techniques</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 71

While Kassi shares her greatest discoveries with the entire medical community, some of her techniques are either still experimental or extremely difficult to perform. Any students who Kassi takes on gain access to her techniques.

**Related Feats**: [Paragon Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=2171)
name: Medical Researcher

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 7"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Paragon Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=2171) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 72

**Archetype** [Pure Legion Enforcer](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=377) (Level 7)

**Prerequisites**
[Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760); Master in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9)

</column>

---

Kassi Aziril taught you her techniques that originated the modern use of [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=760). When you successfully use Battle Medicine, you can also reduce the target's [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=34), [enfeebled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=13), or [clumsy](/Conditions.aspx?ID=3) condition by 1 (this has no effect if you are subject to an effect continually applying the clumsy condition, like [_enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102)). If you are legendary in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9), you can choose to reduce the target's [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19) or [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36) condition by 1 instead, and if you have the [Godless Healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=869) feat, you can choose to reduce the target's [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) or [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=10) condition by 1 instead. If you have the [Mortal Healing](/Feats.aspx?ID=1181) feat, you can reduce all available conditions by 1 for a target who hasn't benefited from positive or healing magic in the last 24 hours, and if you roll a critical success before applying the effects of the feat, you reduce all available conditions by 2 for that target instead.

<title level="2">Kassi's Techniques</title>

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 71

While Kassi shares her greatest discoveries with the entire medical community, some of her techniques are either still experimental or extremely difficult to perform. Any students who Kassi takes on gain access to her techniques.

**Related Feats**: [Medical Researcher](/Feats.aspx?ID=2170)
name: Paragon Battle Medicine

<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />

<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #172: Secrets of the Temple City](/Sources.aspx?ID=102) pg. 79, [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Bright Lion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=129)

**Prerequisites**
member of the Bright Lions; not a worshipper of [Walkena](/Deities.aspx?ID=159); Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

**Access**
 Bright Lion  background

</column>

---

You become trained in your choice of [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) or [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) and in [Mzali Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8); if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. You incorporate enough worship of Walkena in your daily life to avoid suspicion. You typically don't need to [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) or [Impersonate](/Actions.aspx?ID=46) to pass yourself off as a worshipper of [Walkena](/Deities.aspx?ID=159). Against careful inspection, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to Deception checks specifically to pass yourself off as a version of yourself faithful to Walkena.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the Bright Lion archetype.

<title level="2">Bright Lion Dedication leads to...</title>
[Blessing of the Sun Gods](/Feats.aspx?ID=3410), [Elude the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=2176), [Lion's Might](/Feats.aspx?ID=3411), [Sun's Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=2174), [Warding Light](/Feats.aspx?ID=2173)
name: Bright Lion Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: member of the Bright Lions; not a worshipper of [Walkena](/Deities.aspx?ID=159); Trained in [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Warding Light](/Feats.aspx?ID=2173) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Bright Lion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=129)

**Prerequisites**
[Bright Lion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2172)

</column>

---

You gain a tattoo that serves as a special ward to defend you against [Walkena's](/Deities.aspx?ID=159) forces. The tattoo allows you to cast the [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=171) cantrip as a divine innate spell at will. The cantrip is heightened to a spell level equal to half your level rounded up. If you die, the tattoo immediately casts [_gentle repose_](/Spells.aspx?ID=131) on your corpse, heightened to the same spell level.
name: Warding Light

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Sun's Fury](/Feats.aspx?ID=2174) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Bright Lion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=129)

**Prerequisites**
[Bright Lion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2172)

</column>

---

The [Old Sun Gods](/DeityCategories.aspx?ID=19) have granted you a gift in your fight against [Walkena](/Deities.aspx?ID=159). You gain the [_sun's fury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=818) focus spell. If you don't already have one, you gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point, which you can regain using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity to pray to the Old Sun Gods; if you already have a focus pool, increase the number of Focus Points in your focus pool by 1. Your Bright Lion focus spells are divine spells; when you gain this feat, if you aren't already, you become trained in divine spell attacks and spell DCs, using Charisma as your spellcasting ability score.
name: Sun's Fury

    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Elude the Divine](/Feats.aspx?ID=2176) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Legends](/Sources.aspx?ID=40) pg. 101

**Archetype** [Bright Lion](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=129)

**Prerequisites**
[Bright Lion Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2172)

</column>

---

You are able to escape inquisitions involving divine magic, allowing you to blend in well in Mzali but also to avoid unwanted attention in other oppressive theocracies. You gain the [Slippery Secrets](/Feats.aspx?ID=842) skill feat, and its benefits also apply against [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47) effects that attempt to discern your deity. When using Slippery Secrets against a divine divination, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus, and on a critical success, the divination reveals false information appropriate to your false persona, rather than nothing.
name: Elude the Divine

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Oozemorph Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2185) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from The Slithering</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [The Slithering](/Sources.aspx?ID=41) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Oozemorph](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=84)

</column>

---

You are plagued by sporadic anatomical rearrangements, which grant you insights into other creatures with unusual anatomies. You become trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) and [Ooze Lore](/Traits.aspx?ID=122); if you were already trained, you become an expert instead. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Reflex saves to avoid being [Engulfed](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=14) by creatures, and to checks to [Escape](/Actions.aspx?ID=79) after being Engulfed.

 As you develop more abilities based on your amorphous affliction, you become increasingly off-putting to mundane creatures. You take a penalty to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks against [animals](/Traits.aspx?ID=9) and [humanoids](/Traits.aspx?ID=91). This penalty is equal to your number of class feats from the oozemorph archetype, to a maximum of –4 for four or more feats.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the oozemorph archetype.

<title level="2">Oozemorph Dedication leads to...</title>
[Disturbing Defense](/Feats.aspx?ID=2186), [Hideous Ululation](/Feats.aspx?ID=2187), [Ooze Empathy](/Feats.aspx?ID=2189), [Uncanny Suction](/Feats.aspx?ID=2191), [Vacate Vision](/Feats.aspx?ID=2188)
name: Oozemorph Dedication

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [The Slithering](/Sources.aspx?ID=41) pg. 59

**Archetype** [Oozemorph](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=84)

**Prerequisites**
[Oozemorph Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2185)

**Trigger**
You fail (but don't critically fail) a saving throw against an effect with the [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=163) trait.

</column>

---

You cause your eyes to liquefy and run out of your eye sockets, leaving them empty. You get a success on the save against the triggering effect, but you are [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=1) for the duration the effect would have had on a failure. At that time, your eyes regrow and your normal vision returns.
name: Vacate Vision

You can connect with [oozes](/Traits.aspx?ID=122) (and other amorphous [aberrations](/Traits.aspx?ID=1), at the GM's discretion) on a rudimentary level. You can use [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) on oozes and to make very simple [Requests](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) of them. In most instances, oozes will give you time to make your case.

Your skin develops a malleable, rubbery quality. You gain resistance to bludgeoning damage equal to 2 + your number of class feats from the oozemorph archetype. You also gain resistance equal to twice that amount to extra damage from critical hits; if the resistance is greater than the extra damage, it reduces the extra damage to 0 but doesn't reduce the attack's normal damage.

Your limbs produce strange, sucker-like growths that allow you to adhere to walls, objects, and most other surfaces. You gain a climb Speed equal to your half your land Speed (minimum 5 feet). In addition, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your Reflex DC to avoid being [Disarmed](/Actions.aspx?ID=41).

 In addition, your resistance to precision damage from Disturbing Defense increases to an amount equal to 7 + your number of class feats from the oozemorph archetype, and the DC of your flat checks to recover from [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29) is 8 rather than 13.

markdown:
**Source** [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 79 **Archetype** [Edgewatch Detective](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=85) **Prerequisites** [Edgewatch Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2193) --- You read the body language of an intelligent creature within 30 feet and pick up on subtle cues to detect its general propensity for good or evil and law or chaos. The GM rolls a [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) Perception check, comparing the result against the target's [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) DC. **Critical Success** You ascertain the target's alignment.
**Success** You ascertain the target's alignment, but only along one axis (your choice of either the good-evil axis or the lawful-chaotic axis).
**Failure** You do not learn the target's alignment.
**Critical Failure** You incorrectly ascertain the target's alignment, misidentifying their alignment along either one axis or both (GM's choice).
name: Sense Alignment

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Edgewatch Detective](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=85)

**Prerequisites**
[Edgewatch Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2193)

</column>

---

You can sense the presence of the supernatural, even if you don't have any magical ability yourself. You can spend a single action (this action has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait) to determine if any magical auras are present in the area. This has the effects of a 1st-level [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) spell but isn't magical. By straining, you can also discover the source of the magic and its school (with the effect of a 4th-level _detect magic_ spell), but doing so prevents you from using this ability again until you've rested for 8 hours.
name: Arcane Sensitivity

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs=""
>
[Bolera's Interrogation](/Feats.aspx?ID=2196) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #157: Devil at the Dreaming Palace](/Sources.aspx?ID=45) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Edgewatch Detective](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=85)

**Frequency**
three times per day

**Prerequisites**
[Edgewatch Detective Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2193)

</column>

---

You focus your attention on a single creature within 30 feet. That creature must attempt a Will save against your Perception DC. On a failure, the creature finds itself unable to speak any deliberate and intentional lies and takes a –2 penalty to [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) checks. On a critical failure, the penalty to Deception checks is –4. This effect lasts for 10 minutes as long as you are within 30 feet of the target and the target is aware that you are using the ability against it. If you begin a new interrogation, this effect ends for any previous target. After your interrogation ends, the target is immune to this ability for 24 hours.
name: Bolera's Interrogation

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Jalmeri Heavenseeker Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2198) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Impossible Lands](/Sources.aspx?ID=146) pg. 224

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The Jalmeri Heavenseeker archetype as printed in Lost Omens: Impossible Lands is legal for play. Players may not use the version printed in Pathfinder #158: Six Feet Under._

**Archetype** [Jalmeri Heavenseeker](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=198)

**Prerequisites**
[Student of Perfection Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=900), or you've trained with a champion of the Challenge of Sky and Heaven

</column>

---

You become trained in either [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) or [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11); if you were already trained in both these skills, you become an expert in one of them instead. You gain either the [Ki Rush](/Feats.aspx?ID=432) or [Ki Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=433) monk feat, which grants you a ki spell and a focus pool of 1 Focus Point that you can recover using the [Refocus](/Actions.aspx?ID=71) activity as a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=8) does. If you already have both feats, you can instead choose a single 1st-level [monk feat](/Feats.aspx?Traits=112).

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you gain two other feats from the Jalmeri Heavenseeker or [Student of Perfection](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=18) archetypes.

<title level="2">Jalmeri Heavenseeker Dedication leads to...</title>
[Heaven's Thunder](/Feats.aspx?ID=2199), [Sky and Heaven Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2200), [Speaking Sky](/Feats.aspx?ID=2204), [Steal the Sky](/Feats.aspx?ID=2202)
name: Jalmeri Heavenseeker Dedication
prerequisite_markdown: [Student of Perfection Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=900), or you've trained with a champion of the Challenge of Sky and Heaven

<trait label="Electricity" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=56" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />

</column>

---

Until the end of your next turn, your [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) attacks and any [monk](/Traits.aspx?ID=112) weapons you wield deal additional electricity and sonic damage; the amount of each damage type is equal to the number of damage dice for the weapon or unarmed attack. Any creature who successfully [Grapples](/Actions.aspx?ID=35) you or is successfully Grappled by you takes this damage immediately after the Grapple check is resolved (using the number of damage dice of your unarmed attack to determine the damage dealt).

 If you also know the [_ki blast_](/Spells.aspx?ID=484) ki spell, you can choose to deal either electricity or sonic damage with the spell instead of force while this effect is active (_ki blast_ loses the [force](/Traits.aspx?ID=75) trait and gains the [electricity](/Traits.aspx?ID=56) or [sonic](/Traits.aspx?ID=147) trait instead).
name: Heaven's Thunder

</title>

</column>

---

Your jagged blows are like lightning. The only Strikes you can make are skyward slash unarmed attacks. These unarmed attacks deal 1d10 slashing damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199), and [versatile P](/Traits.aspx?ID=200) traits. While in Sky and Heaven Stance, you gain resistance 2 to electricity and resistance 2 to sonic damage. These resistances increase to 5 at 12th level and to 8 at 18th level.

<title level="2">Sky and Heaven Stance leads to...</title>
[Cross the Final Horizon](/Feats.aspx?ID=2205), [Skyseeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=2201)

</column>

---

You [Leap](/Actions.aspx?ID=81) toward an opponent. At any point in the Leap, you can make a single Strike against a creature within your reach. At 12th level, if you hit with this Strike, instead of finishing your Leap, you can immediately attempt a second Leap from the space where you struck the target of your Strike. You can make a single Strike against a target within your reach during this second Leap as well. At 16th level, you can attempt a third Leap if your second Strike hits, and you can attack a target at any point during this third Leap. All Leaps made during this activity gain a +10-foot status bonus to the total height or distance of your leap, and no two Strikes can target the same creature.
name: Skyseeker
