</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Agents of Edgewatch</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Flourish" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=73" />
<trait label="Manipulate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=104" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #159: All or Nothing](/Sources.aspx?ID=48) pg. 75

**Access**
Help either the Washboard Dogs or the Diobel Sweepers destroy their rival.

**Requirements**
You're wielding a melee weapon that deals slashing damage and you have a free hand.

</column>

---

You attempt a melee Strike with the required weapon. If you hit and deal damage and the result of your attack roll exceeds the target's Perception DC, you [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) a small object from the target (as if you had succeeded at that task using the [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) skill). Depending on the object, the target might realize that it has been stolen, and onlookers might realize you have stolen the object if the attack roll doesn't exceed their Perception DCs.

<title level="2">Gang Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #159: All or Nothing](/Sources.aspx?ID=48) pg. 75

Street gangs in Absalom recruit amateur pickpockets, ruffians, and vandals who show promise in their specialties and operate well in tight groups.

 Rogue agents can gain access to some of the following rogue feats by successfully helping Maurrisa Jonne or Bloody Berleth eliminate their rival (see Chapter 1). Some of these feats, however, can be gained only by helping a particular side in the gang conflict.

**Related Feats**: [Ambushing Knockdown](/Feats.aspx?ID=2207), [Coordinated Distraction](/Feats.aspx?ID=2210), [Deny Support](/Feats.aspx?ID=2209), [Subtle Shank](/Feats.aspx?ID=2208)

<traits>

---

You emerge from hiding and deliver a blow that can knock an enemy sprawling to the ground. You Stride up to half your Speed, but you must end your movement next to a foe you're hidden from or undetected by. You have a melee weapon of light bulk [Concealed](/Actions.aspx?ID=61) on your person via the [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15) skill, and the target isn't aware of it.

</column>

---

You draw a concealed weapon, attack with it, and then hide it away again in one smooth motion. Interact to draw the weapon, Strike with that weapon, and then Conceal it with a Stealth check. The target is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=16) for this attack.

<title level="2">Gang Feats</title>

**Source** [Pathfinder #159: All or Nothing](/Sources.aspx?ID=48) pg. 75

Street gangs in Absalom recruit amateur pickpockets, ruffians, and vandals who show promise in their specialties and operate well in tight groups.

 Rogue agents can gain access to some of the following rogue feats by successfully helping Maurrisa Jonne or Bloody Berleth eliminate their rival (see Chapter 1). Some of these feats, however, can be gained only by helping a particular side in the gang conflict.

[Provocator Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2211) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Agents of Edgewatch</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #159: All or Nothing](/Sources.aspx?ID=48) pg. 79

**Archetype** [Provocator](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=87)

**Prerequisites**
Strength 14; Charisma 14

**Access**
Successfully defeat the  eberarks  in the Irorium.

</column>

---

For you, fighting is as much about style as skill. You become trained in simple weapons, martial weapons, and one advanced weapon of your choice. When you critically succeed at an attack roll using your chosen advanced weapon, you also apply the critical specialization of that weapon. You become an expert in [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=12) and your choice of either [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=1) or [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3). If you were an expert in all three of these skills, you instead become an expert in a skill of your choice.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the provocator archetype.

<title level="2">Provocator Dedication leads to...</title>
[Cartwheel Dodge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2213), [Flourishing Finish](/Feats.aspx?ID=2212), [Murderer's Circle](/Feats.aspx?ID=2214), [Performance Weapon Expert](/Feats.aspx?ID=2216), [Pin to the Spot](/Feats.aspx?ID=2215)
name: Provocator Dedication

<title

    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Archaeologist's Warning](/Feats.aspx?ID=2225) <actions string="Free Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13)

**Trigger**
You are about to roll a Perception, [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14), or [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check for initiative.

name: Archaeologist's Warning

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Craft Facsimile](/Feats.aspx?ID=2226) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 40

**Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13)

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4); [Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871)

</column>

---

You're renowned for your ability to recover important items, whether they're from a long-abandoned temple or a thieves' den, and sometimes that requires creating a stand-in. You can spend 1 minute to assemble a facsimile of an object of no more than 1 Bulk that you can see from available materials. The facsimile has the same shape and Bulk as the replaced object and can pass as the object if not examined. A creature familiar with the original who examines the object automatically realizes that it is a fake. A creature not familiar with the original can still tell it was a hastily jury-rigged stand-in of some kind, but they might not know the object was supposed to be something else.
name: Craft Facsimile

markdown:
**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 41 **Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13) **Prerequisites** Master in [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9); [Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871) --- Pathfinders are trained to help each other be safe and successful, and you know how to jolt your allies back to their senses. Attempt a [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9) check on an adjacent ally who is [fascinated](/Conditions.aspx?ID=14), [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=19), [stunned](/Conditions.aspx?ID=36), or [stupefied](/Conditions.aspx?ID=37) and choose one of those conditions. The DC is the DC for the effect that caused the condition. **Critical Success** Reduce the value of the chosen condition by 2. If you chose fascinated, that condition ends.
**Success** Reduce the value of the chosen condition by 1. If you chose fascinated, that condition ends.
**Failure** The target is unaffected.
**Critical Failure** You increase the value of the chosen condition by 1. If you chose fascinated, increase the duration by 1 round instead.
name: Snap Out of It! (Pathfinder Agent)

<traits>

</column>

---

You are adept at using the unusual resonance of your [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470) to aid in any deceptions you might employ. Once per day you can activate your _wayfinder_ to cast [_illusory disguise_](/Spells.aspx?ID=159), heightened to 2nd level, as an arcane spell. Additionally, once per day, you can Activate your _wayfinder_ with a single-action envision activation to transform it into a perfect replica of another similarly sized badge of membership or office, such as an Aspis Consortium badge. This transformation lasts for 1 hour.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidetic Memorization](/Feats.aspx?ID=2230) <actions string="Three Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 41

**Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13)

**Prerequisites**
[Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871)

</column>

---

Pathfinders are often asked to study the intricacies of ancient history under less than ideal conditions, and you've trained your brain to make the most of your limited time. You quickly memorize an inscription, scroll, or other piece of writing you can clearly see whose length is equivalent to no more than one page. You can then accurately recreate this writing using a [writing kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=63), wax tablet, or other writing medium, once you have reached safety. You don't need to understand the writing or identify the language to reproduce it. Your memory lasts until the next time you make your daily preparations; if you have not written down or otherwise recreated what you saw by this point, your memory fades and any attempt to recreate what you saw is garbled and unintelligible. You can have only one piece of writing memorized at a time; any previous memorization fades the next time you use this ability.
name: Eidetic Memorization

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 44 **Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31) **Prerequisites** [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130) --- You feel confident that you can explain your scrolls so well that even your allies can use them... hopefully. During your daily preparations, you can select a single scroll and instruct a single ally in its use. Until your next daily preparations, the ally can attempt to use the scroll with a single action that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=104) trait. The ally rolls the skill corresponding to the spellcasting tradition. The GM determines the DC based on the scroll's level, possibly adjusted for the situation. If the spell has a spell attack bonus or a spell DC, use your level as the proficiency bonus and the highest of your ally's Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma modifiers. **Success** For the rest of the ally's turn, they can spend actions to Cast the Spell.
**Failure** The ally misremembers your instructions and can't use the scroll this turn.
**Critical Failure** The ally misunderstood your instructions and can't use the scroll.
name: Foolproof Instructions

    pfs="Standard"

[Magic Finder](/Feats.aspx?ID=2234) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

</column>

---

You've learned a few tricks to ensure you find every last magic item and interesting magical effect during your missions. You can cast [_detect magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=66) and [_read aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=246) as innate spells at will. You can also cast [_locate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=173) as an innate spell once per day, but only to locate magic items, not specific mundane objects or types of objects. If you could already cast spells, these spells are of the same tradition. Otherwise, they're arcane spells, you use Intelligence as your spellcasting ability, and you become trained in spell attack rolls and spell DCs for arcane spells.

 When you cast _detect magic_ or _read aura_ from this feat on an item or creature under the effect of a [_magic aura_](/Spells.aspx?ID=178) spell or interact with an item under the effect of an [_item facade_](/Spells.aspx?ID=166) spell, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus on your check to disbelieve the illusion.
name: Magic Finder

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bestiary Scholar](/Feats.aspx?ID=2236) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 44

**Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31)

**Prerequisites**
Master in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), Master in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Master in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

</column>

---

You learn how to apply your extensive knowledge of certain creatures using the lore you know. When you gain this feat, choose one of the following skills in which you have attained master proficiency: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13). You can use the chosen skill to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) to identify any creature normally identifiable by any of the above skills. In addition, when you critically succeed at identifying a creature, you gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your next attack roll or saving throw against that creature.
name: Bestiary Scholar
prerequisite_markdown: Master in [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), Master in [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), Master in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), Master in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), or Master in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13); [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

You can cast [_dispel magic_](/Spells.aspx?ID=78), [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=168), and [_resist energy_](/Spells.aspx?ID=256) as innate spells, each once per day. markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Masterful Obfuscation](/Feats.aspx?ID=2238) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 44

**Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31)

**Prerequisites**
master in a skill with the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action; [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

</column>

---

You've mastered the art of deceiving others about your area of study. When you gain this feat, choose one skill with the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action in which you have attained master proficiency. You can attempt to deceive others about the truth of facts and knowledge related to that skill—such as by pretending to know something that you don't, planting false information, or the like—using the [Lie](/Actions.aspx?ID=47) action of the [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=5) skill. When you Lie in this way, attempt your skill check using the skill you selected when you gained this feat instead of Deception, applying all bonuses or penalties related from the selected skill to the check. While it's easier to convince someone who knows nothing about your chosen false information, on a critical success, you can even convince someone who has heard something contradictory, unless they have reason to be certain from direct experience. For instance, you might say, “I know you may have heard to use [silver weapons](/Equipment.aspx?ID=379) against [vampires](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=97), but that's a misconception based on conflating them with [werewolves](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=100). According to all credible scholars, [cold iron](/Equipment.aspx?ID=375) will do the trick.” Once a creature realizes you've used masterful obfuscation to Lie to them, either from a failed check or due to later discovering the falsehood, you can't use this feat against them again, as it relies on blind trust in your superior knowledge. However, you can still Lie to them normally.
name: Masterful Obfuscation
prerequisite_markdown: master in a skill with the [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) action; [Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

>
[Determined Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=2239) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 45

**Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31)

**Prerequisites**
[Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=1131)

</column>

---

You've mastered useful spells that let you find hidden truths others might miss, and a preferred spell comes naturally to you. When you gain this feat, choose one of the innate spells granted by the [Lore Seeker](/Feats.aspx?ID=1131) feat: [_comprehend language_](/Spells.aspx?ID=46), [_restore senses_](/Spells.aspx?ID=259), or [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=271). You can cast this spell three times per day instead of once per day.

 When you cast any of the spells granted by Lore Seeker as innate spells, they gain additional effects. When you cast _comprehend language_, the spell is heightened to 4th level. When you cast _restore senses_, the spell is heightened to half your level, rounded up. When you cast _see invisibility_, for the duration of the spell you can use an action, which has the [concentrate](/Traits.aspx?ID=32) trait, to see invisible creatures normally (instead of seeing them as translucent shapes that are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to you) until the end of your next turn.
name: Determined Lore Seeker

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ward Mind](/Feats.aspx?ID=2240) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 45

**Archetype** [Scrollmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=31)

**Prerequisites**
[Scrollmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1130)

**Trigger**
Your turn ends.

**Requirements**
You're currently affected by a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect that you gained by failing, but not critically failing, a saving throw.

</column>

---

To protect the knowledge within your mind from intrusion or erasure, you ward yourself against a mental attack, temporarily delaying its effects. Until the end of your next turn, you ignore a single mental effect that meets the requirement. You can suppress a particular effect using Ward Mind or [Cognitive Loophole](/Feats.aspx?ID=593) only once, even if you have both feats.

**Special** You can use this reaction even if the mental effect prevents you from using reactions.
name: Ward Mind
requirement_markdown: You're currently affected by a [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effect that you gained by failing, but not critically failing, a saving throw.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Glyph Expert](/Feats.aspx?ID=2242) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Spellmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=32)

**Prerequisites**
[Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134)

</column>

---

You've learned to recognize the telltale writings and other signs that indicate the presence of magical traps. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks to find magical traps, to AC against attacks made by magical traps, to saves against magical traps, and to checks to [Disable](/Actions.aspx?ID=69) magical traps. Even if you aren't [Searching](/Actions.aspx?ID=519), you get a check to find magical traps that normally require you to be Searching. You still need to meet any other requirements to find the trap. 

You're also adept at avoiding the effects of traps that feature magical writing, such as runes, glyphs, ancient script, or other forms. When you attempt a saving throw against the effects of these traps, if you roll a success, you get a critical success instead. If you roll a critical failure, you get a failure instead.
name: Glyph Expert

    right="Feat 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Recover Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2243) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />
<trait label="Metamagic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=107" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 48

**Archetype** [Spellmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=32)

**Prerequisites**
[Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134)

**Requirements**
An enemy's action disrupted your attempts to Cast a Spell since the beginning of your last turn.

</column>

---

You recover the energy of a recently disrupted spell, allowing you to cast it again as long as you do so immediately before the energy dissipates. You regain the use of a spell that was disrupted since the beginning of your last turn, but you must immediately Cast the Spell again with your next action or else you lose the spell; as normal for metamagic, this must be on the same turn that you use Recover Spell. You can change the area, targets, and other variables, including the number of actions used to Cast the Spell if the spell allows, such as when casting [_heal_](/Spells.aspx?ID=148) or [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=180).
name: Recover Spell
requirement_markdown: An enemy's action disrupted your attempts to Cast a Spell since the beginning of your last turn.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 16"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Master's Counterspell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2247) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 48

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The trigger for Master’s Counterspell should begin with “A creature Casts a Spell that you have prepared or is in your repertoire...” to provide a trigger for spontaneous spellcasters as well as prepared spellcasters._

**Archetype** [Spellmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=32)

**Prerequisites**
[Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134); master in spell attack rolls and spell DCs

**Trigger**
A creature Casts a Spell that you have prepared, and that spell was cast or created with a tradition of magic in which you have master proficiency in spell attack rolls and spell DCs.

</column>

---

When a foe Casts a Spell and you can see its manifestations, you can use your magic to disrupt it. If you're a prepared caster, you can expend a prepared spell to counter the triggering creature's casting of a spell. If you're a spontaneous caster, you can expend a spell slot. The level of the prepared spell or spell slot must be at least as high as the triggering spell's level. You lose your prepared spell or spell slot as if you had Cast the Spell. You then attempt to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) the triggering spell, and you take a –2 penalty to your counteract check; for prepared casters, the GM might waive the penalty if the spell you're expending to counterspell is especially appropriate—such as countering with the same spell, or using a [_cone of cold_](/Spells.aspx?ID=47) to counteract a [_fireball_](/Spells.aspx?ID=119).
name: Master's Counterspell
prerequisite_markdown: [Spellmaster Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=1134); master in spell attack rolls and spell DCs

</column>

---

You dart around the battlefield, casting spells while you carefully make melee attacks with your weapon. Stride once. If you end your movement within melee reach of at least one enemy, you can make a melee Strike against that enemy. You can use Mobile Magical Combat while [Burrowing](/Actions.aspx?ID=93), [Climbing](/Actions.aspx?ID=33), [Flying](/Actions.aspx?ID=94), or [Swimming](/Actions.aspx?ID=39) instead of Striding if you have the corresponding movement type.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 18"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Retain Absorbed Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=2249) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 49

**Archetype** [Spellmaster](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=32)

**Prerequisites**
[Absorb Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1138)

</column>

---

When you absorb a spell using the [Absorb Spell](/Feats.aspx?ID=1138) reaction, you utilize secret, techniques that allow you to retain the power and knowledge necessary to cast the spell repeatedly—potentially for a greater period of time. If you're a spontaneous caster, you can cast the triggering spell up to three times instead of once. If you're a prepared spellcaster, you can replace up to three of your prepared spells of the same level with the absorbed spell instead of one. Either way, you can choose to retain the absorbed spell for the next hour instead of the next 10 minutes; if you choose to extend the absorption to an hour, you can't use Absorb Spell again until one hour after the spell is absorbed.
name: Retain Absorbed Spell

You can attempt to [Repair](/Actions.aspx?ID=42) an item without a [repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=43), instead improvising tools out of your gear or found items. This adds 5 minutes to the time it takes to Repair the item. You can also Repair an item while on the move without needing a stable surface. This adds 10 minutes to the times it takes to Repair the item.

</column>

---

You've learned a variety of methods for helping allies who are bleeding, burning, or otherwise suffering persistent damage, from brushing off acid to patting down fires. Your ally immediately attempts a flat check to remove all types of persistent damage, and your use of this feat automatically counts as especially effective aid, reducing the DC from 15 to 10 for all future flat checks to remove the persistent damage.
name: Emergency Medical Assistance
requirement_markdown: An adjacent ally is suffering [persistent damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).

---

You've studied survival skills with the School of Swords experts and are comfortable spending long periods of time in the wilderness. You don't get fatigued more quickly due to precipitation during overland travel, though you can otherwise be fatigued as normal. You can go 3 days + your Constitution modifier without water before you take damage from thirst and you can go 5 days + your Constitution modifier without food before you take damage from [starvation](/Rules.aspx?ID=537).
name: Rugged Survivalist

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fane's Fourberie](/Feats.aspx?ID=2266) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=321" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this feat. A character with the Fane’s Fourberie feat can etch their deck of cards with weapon runes themself or have this service provided by a third party. The deck can also be targeted by weapon-modifying effects such as a magic weapon spell._

**Access**
Member of the Pathfinder Society.

</column>

---

Stella Fane has perfected a technique for using playing cards as weapons that's one part sleight of hand and one part magic. When you enter this stance, choose whether to treat [playing cards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=873) in your possession as [daggers](/Weapons.aspx?ID=3) or [darts](/Weapons.aspx?ID=68); you can wield a playing card in all ways as the chosen weapon until the stance ends. As long as the majority of the deck remains in your possession, any cards that are lost or thrown can be found after 1 minute. Otherwise, the remaining cards are likely lost or destroyed.

 A character who has this feat can enchant a single deck of playing cards as a magic weapon, etching fundamental and property runes directly onto the deck of cards.

<title level="2">Perilous Larceny</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

Pirate captain Stella Fane (CN female human privateer; _Lost Omens Character Guide_ 119) joined the Pathfinder Society as a venture-captain as part of a deal to help the Society make it through the heavily armed naval blockades of Port Peril's Free Captains. Cunning and competitive, Stella's greatest weakness as a leader of the Society is her uncompromising hatred of the Chelaxian government. Those who can avoid ending up sunk or executed by Chelaxian naval commanders while assisting Stella may leave with a few more tricks than they had before seeing the skilled pirate in action. Fane's Fourberie and Stella's Stab and Snag, below, are class feats that rogues or swashbucklers can select. When you select either feat, it loses the trait from the other class.

**Related Feats**: [Fane's Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=2267), [Stella's Stab and Snag](/Feats.aspx?ID=2268)
name: Fane's Fourberie

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Fane's Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=2267) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

**Prerequisites**

**Access**
Member of the Pathfinder Society.

**Requirements**
You have a deck of [playing cards](/Equipment.aspx?ID=873) in your possession.

</column>

---

A good getaway requires a good distraction. You Interact to draw a deck of cards and flip it in a fluttering explosion, causing you to become briefly [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to all other creatures using vision as their precise sense, and then you [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63). You have enough cover to Sneak until the end of your turn. This leaves all of the cards from the deck scattered about the room; collecting them into a deck again takes several minutes.

<title level="2">Perilous Larceny</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

Pirate captain Stella Fane (CN female human privateer; _Lost Omens Character Guide_ 119) joined the Pathfinder Society as a venture-captain as part of a deal to help the Society make it through the heavily armed naval blockades of Port Peril's Free Captains. Cunning and competitive, Stella's greatest weakness as a leader of the Society is her uncompromising hatred of the Chelaxian government. Those who can avoid ending up sunk or executed by Chelaxian naval commanders while assisting Stella may leave with a few more tricks than they had before seeing the skilled pirate in action. Fane's Fourberie and Stella's Stab and Snag, below, are class feats that rogues or swashbucklers can select. When you select either feat, it loses the trait from the other class.

**Related Feats**: [Fane's Fourberie](/Feats.aspx?ID=2266), [Stella's Stab and Snag](/Feats.aspx?ID=2268)

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Stella's Stab and Snag](/Feats.aspx?ID=2268) <actions string="Two Actions" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Rogue" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=139" />
<trait label="Swashbuckler" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=321" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this feat._

**Prerequisites**
Expert in [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17)

**Access**
Member of the Pathfinder Society.

**Requirements**
You are wielding a piercing or slashing melee weapon.

</column>

---

You dart towards your opponent, seeking to stab them and slice their purse-strings with a single movement. Stride up to your Speed; this movement does not trigger reactions triggered by movement. At any point during this movement you can Strike an opponent within reach. If this Strike is successful, you can attempt a [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=17) check to [Steal](/Actions.aspx?ID=68) from the target.

<title level="2">Perilous Larceny</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

Pirate captain Stella Fane (CN female human privateer; _Lost Omens Character Guide_ 119) joined the Pathfinder Society as a venture-captain as part of a deal to help the Society make it through the heavily armed naval blockades of Port Peril's Free Captains. Cunning and competitive, Stella's greatest weakness as a leader of the Society is her uncompromising hatred of the Chelaxian government. Those who can avoid ending up sunk or executed by Chelaxian naval commanders while assisting Stella may leave with a few more tricks than they had before seeing the skilled pirate in action. Fane's Fourberie and Stella's Stab and Snag, below, are class feats that rogues or swashbucklers can select. When you select either feat, it loses the trait from the other class.

**Related Feats**: [Fane's Escape](/Feats.aspx?ID=2267), [Fane's Fourberie](/Feats.aspx?ID=2266)
name: Stella's Stab and Snag
requirement_markdown: You are wielding a piercing or slashing melee weapon.

    level="1"
    right="Feat 1"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Rain of Embers Stance](/Feats.aspx?ID=2269) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Fire" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=72" />
<trait label="Monk" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=112" />
<trait label="Stance" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=152" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118, [PFS Quest #2: Unforgiving Fire](/Sources.aspx?ID=11) pg. 20

**Requirements**
You are unarmored.

</column>

---

You enter the stance of an enraged phoenix, holding your fingers as rigid as deadly talons while moving with quick, flickering gestures that dance with biting flames. The only Strikes you can make are fire talon Strikes. These deal 1d4 fire damage; are in the [brawling](/WeaponGroups.aspx?ID=4) group; and have the [agile](/Traits.aspx?ID=170), [finesse](/Traits.aspx?ID=179), [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72), [nonlethal](/Traits.aspx?ID=188), and [unarmed](/Traits.aspx?ID=199) traits.

 While in Rain of Embers Stance, you gain a +1 status bonus to AC and fire resistance equal to half your level (minimum 1).

<title level="2">Rain of Embers Stance leads to...</title>
[Blazing Talon Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2270)

<title level="2">Rain of Embers Style</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 118

The martial artist Meleeka Sanvara (CN female human monk) originally met agents of the Pathfinder Society as an enemy when the agents were sent to retrieve an artifact she had stolen. Now, Meleeka works as a Pathfinder agent and occasionally shares the knowledge of her family's unique martial arts techniques with agents who earn her trust.

**Related Feats**: [Blazing Talon Surge](/Feats.aspx?ID=2270), [Explosive Death Drop](/Feats.aspx?ID=2271)
name: Rain of Embers Stance
requirement_markdown: You are unarmored.

Roll an [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=3) check against your target's Fortitude DC. You take a –1 circumstance penalty to your check if the target is one size larger than you and a –3 circumstance penalty if it's larger than that. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to your check if the target is one size smaller than you and a +3 circumstance bonus if it's smaller than that. **Critical Success** The target takes 12d6 fire damage and falls [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=31) in an unoccupied adjacent square of your choice. The target is no longer grabbed. The target and any creature adjacent to it also each take 1d6 [persistent fire damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29).
**Success** As critical success, but the target takes 6d6 fire damage instead of 12d6, and creatures don't take persistent fire damage.
**Failure** The target is no longer grabbed.
**Critical Failure** As failure, but you fall prone.     right="Feat 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Kreighton's Cognitive Crossover](/Feats.aspx?ID=2272) <actions string="Reaction" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="General" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=78" />
<trait label="Skill" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=144" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) All characters have access to this feat._

**Access**
Member of the Pathfinder Society.

**Trigger**
You gain no information from a [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) check (usually because you failed the [secret](/Traits.aspx?ID=142) check) using one of the two skills you chose for this feat. 

</column>

---

Kreighton teaches his students that an agile mind can glean clues leading to the truth, even from the most unlikely of sources. You immediately reattempt the triggering check using the other chosen skill.

**Special** When you gain this feat, choose two of the following skills: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=2), [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=4), [Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) (any one), [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=9), [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13), or [Society](/Skills.aspx?ID=14).

<title level="2">Scrolls Training</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

The Society's Master of Scrolls, Kreighton Shaine (_Lost Omens Pathfinder Society Guide_ page 42), may be considered strange and absent-minded by his peers, but the elf venture-captain takes his duties quite seriously. Skilled in researching obscure subjects and unraveling inscrutable secrets, Kreighton teaches his students the value of an inquisitive mind.

**Related Feats**:
name: Kreighton's Cognitive Crossover

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Westyr's Wayfinder Repository](/Feats.aspx?ID=2273) <actions string="" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

**Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13)

**Prerequisites**
[Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871); [Wayfinder Resonance Tinkerer](/Feats.aspx?ID=875)

**Requirements**
You have at least one 1st-level spell slot and a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470).

</column>

---

You can use your _wayfinder_ to store a little extra magic for when you need it most. When making your daily preparations, you can choose one 1st-level spell from your spellbook or spell repertoire and store it in your _wayfinder_. You can cast this spell from the _wayfinder_ using the normal actions required. Once the spell has been cast, it is expended, and you must make your next daily preparations to store another spell in your _wayfinder_.

<title level="2">Spells Training</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

Sorrina Westyr (_Lost Omens Pathfinder Society Guide_ page 46) took up the mantle of dean of the school of Spells to replace her predecessor Aram Zey, who had originally been her replacement. When Aram Zey was lost to the magic of the extradimensional demiplane known as the Hao Jin Tapestry, Sorrina began teaching an advanced studies course in extradimensional and interplanar phenomena, hoping to both prevent future losses and better prepare her students for the unexpected challenges that may await them in their careers as Pathfinder agents.

**Related Feats**:
name: Westyr's Wayfinder Repository
requirement_markdown: You have at least one 1st-level spell slot and a [_wayfinder_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=470).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Discerning Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2274) <actions string="Single Action" />
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

**Archetype** [Pathfinder Agent](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=13)

**Prerequisites**
[Pathfinder Agent Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=871); [Thorough Reports](/Feats.aspx?ID=874)

**Requirements**
You have successfully identified the target with [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=26) during this combat.

</column>

---

Identifying a creature is the first step in finding its weaknesses. Attempt a Strike against the target; if successful, you deal an additional 1d6 precision damage. If you have succeeded on a Recall Knowledge check against the target during this combat and their creature type was previously recorded using [Thorough Reports](/Feats.aspx?ID=874), you also deal 1 point of [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=29). The creature is then temporarily immune to Discerning Strike for 1 day.

<title level="2">Swords Training</title>

**Source** [PFS Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=50) pg. 119

Marcos Farabellus (_Lost Omens Pathfinder Society Guide_ page 50) is a talented swordsman and likely the most beloved of the Society's three deans. In addition to teaching his students about the dangers of exploring ruins through energetic and performative lectures, Farabellus also teaches his favorite students a few of the martial techniques he's developed over his illustrious career. The Farabellus Flip is a class feat for barbarians, fighters, or rangers. When you select the feat, it loses the traits from the other classes.

**Related Feats**: [Farabellus Flip](/Feats.aspx?ID=2275)

**Access**
Member of the Pathfinder Society.

**Trigger**

</column>

---

Surprisingly agile even in a breastplate and carrying a full rucksack, Marcos Farabellus developed this evasive maneuver during one of his many adventures. Flinging your body into a twisting somersault, you gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your AC against the triggering attack. If the attack still hits you, you can Step to an open space that's still within the triggering enemy's reach, turning the impact of the blow into momentum.

The Farabellus Flip is a class feat for barbarians, fighters, or rangers. When you select the feat, it loses the traits from the other classes.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Feat 2"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Ghost Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2277) <actions string="" />
</title>

<spoilers>May contain spoilers from Abomination Vaults</spoilers>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
<trait label="Dedication" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=41" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Pathfinder #163: Ruins of Gauntlight](/Sources.aspx?ID=71) pg. 81

**Archetype** [Ghost Hunter](/Archetypes.aspx?ID=88)

**Prerequisites**
Trained in [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=11) or Trained in [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13)

</column>

---

You have focused your training on hunting and defeating ghosts, spirits, and all manner of haunts. Pick [Spirit Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Haunt Lore; you become trained in this skill. If you were already trained in both skills, you become trained in a new Lore skill of your choice.

 Choose two cantrips from the occult spell list. Each cantrip must have the [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), or [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) trait. You can cast these spells as innate occult spells. You gain access to the Cast a Spell activity if you didn't have it already. You're trained in occult spell attack rolls and spell DCs. You can usually replace material components with somatic components, so you don't need a spell component pouch. Your key spellcasting ability for these spells is Charisma.

**Special** You can't select another dedication feat until you have gained two other feats from the ghost hunter archetype.

<title level="2">Ghost Hunter Dedication leads to...</title>
[Ghost Strike](/Feats.aspx?ID=2281), [Grave's Voice](/Feats.aspx?ID=2278), [Investigate Haunting](/Feats.aspx?ID=2279), [Peer Beyond](/Feats.aspx?ID=2282), [Spirit Spells](/Feats.aspx?ID=2280)

You can use your choice of [Spirit Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Haunt Lore to [Make an Impression](/Actions.aspx?ID=50) or [Request](/Actions.aspx?ID=51) things of [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) creatures. At the GM's discretion, an incorporeal [spirit](/Traits.aspx?ID=149) might be willing to hear you out.
name: Grave's Voice

You also gain a +2 circumstance bonus to skill checks to disable haunts.

At 6th level, you gain a 2nd-level occult spell, and at 8th level, you gain a 3rd-level occult spell. Each of these spells must have the [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47), [enchantment](/Traits.aspx?ID=61), or [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117) trait. You can cast each of these spells once per day.

 At 10th level, you can apply this effect to two weapons instead of one.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Archetype" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=12" />
</traits>

**Prerequisites**
[Ghost Hunter Dedication](/Feats.aspx?ID=2277)

</column>

---

You have uncanny insight into the way [ghosts](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=51) and [haunts](/Traits.aspx?ID=88) manifest, manipulate the mind, and rejuvenate. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=106) effects caused by [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=222) [undead](/Traits.aspx?ID=160) and haunts, and you can roll a [Spirit Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=8) or Haunt Lore check for initiative if you know that an incorporeal undead or a haunt is present.

If you are present when a spirit or haunt is defeated, you instantly gain a flash of insight into how it can be permanently put to rest. Typically, this flash manifests as a vision that presents a quick scene closely linked to the source of the spirit or haunt's creation. Attempt a Spirit Lore or Haunt Lore check, respectively, with a DC appropriate for the spirit or haunt's level. On a success, you gain a hint about how to put the spirit to rest or permanently disable the haunt; on a critical success, you learn the exact method of doing so.
name: Peer Beyond

A [peri's](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1262) roaring spirit burns in your soul. Peris engage in constant struggle to right the wrongs of their fallen forebears, and that struggle has been passed on to you. You have a direct impulse to right wrongs and better the world. You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to saving throws against [fire](/Traits.aspx?ID=72) effects, and your perception is unaffected by non-magical flames, fog and smoke.

[Flame Jump](/Feats.aspx?ID=2294), [Peri Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2292)

Your wild, unique features betray your [agathion](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=216) birthright. Serene celestials from the plane of [Nirvana](/Planes.aspx?ID=20), agathions seek the peaceful path, ensuring a better world for all to coexist. Your halcyon demeanor leads you to search for peaceful resolution to conflict. You gain the trained proficiency rank in [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=10). If you would automatically become trained in Nature (from your background or class, for example), you instead become trained in a skill of your choice. You know the [Sylvan](/Languages.aspx?ID=10) language and gain the [Natural Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=815) skill feat.

<title level="2">Idyllkin leads to...</title>
[Agathion Magic](/Feats.aspx?ID=2289), [Tranquil Sanctuary](/Feats.aspx?ID=2288)

<trait label="Concentrate" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=32" />

Your [peri](/Monsters.aspx?ID=1262) lineage has opened your mind to fire's raw, purging essence. You can spend 1 minute in focused preparation, then enter a fire your size or larger to instantly teleport to any fire that is your size or larger up to 5 miles away. You take no damage from entering or exiting fires when using this ability. Once you enter the fire, you instantly know the rough locations of other large fires within range and can exit from the original fire, if you prefer. You can't carry [extradimensional](/Traits.aspx?ID=67) spaces with you when you teleport; if you attempt to do so, the activity fails.
name: Flame Jump

You call on your celestial forebears to rid your body of all toxins and impurities. For each [disease](/Traits.aspx?ID=46) or [poison](/Traits.aspx?ID=126) currently affecting you, attempt a [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=13) check to [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=371) that disease or poison. As normal, your counteract level is equal to half your level, rounded up.
name: Purge Sins

It is paramount that you are understood and you understand others; language need not impede your message. You can cast [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=340) as a 5th-level divine innate spell once per day on yourself only. You gain a +1 status bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6) checks for the duration of the spell when speaking to creatures with whom you already shared a language before casting.
name: Truespeech

markdown:
**Source** [Ancestry Guide](/Sources.aspx?ID=74) pg. 11 --- Your inherent glory radiates into the world with all the divine power of the celestial planes for 1 minute. Any creature that can see you while you Radiate Glory must attempt a Will saving throw against your class DC or spell DC, whichever is higher. The creature then becomes temporarily immune to this effect for 24 hours. **Critical Success** The creature is unaffected.
**Success** You are [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=4) to the creature for 1 round by the shining light, but can't use this concealment to [Hide](/Actions.aspx?ID=62), as normal for when your location is obvious.
**Failure** As success, except you are concealed to the creature while your glory radiates.
**Critical Failure** You are [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=22) to the creature while your glory radiates, lost amid the radiant corona of your presence. Your general position is still obvious, so you can't use this to [Sneak](/Actions.aspx?ID=63).
name: Radiate Glory
